description: Output-quality evals. Each case is run A/B (with the Pathfinder skill available vs. a baseline agent without it), graded by an LLM judge against discriminating expectations. See evals/README.md.

evals.prompt: I have a Next.js app with Playwright tests in e2e/tests/. Map the user journeys in this codebase and tell me which steps already have test coverage and which are gaps. Create the journey map and a coverage summary.
evals.expected_output: A journeys.json mapping the app's flows with per-step tested/untested status reflecting the existing home.spec.ts, plus the skill's signature colour-coded Mermaid coverage visualization.

expectations.text: A Mermaid coverage visualization is produced (a blazes.md file or Mermaid flowchart) with colour-coded tested vs. untested nodes — the skill's signature decision-tree output — rather than only a prose or table summary.

The journeys.json uses Pathfinder's schema: step ids in PREFIX-NN form (e.g. AUTH-01), and each step has id, action, screen, and a tested value of true/false/"partial". (A free-form structure that downstream scripts could not consume fails this.)
The agent identifies Playwright as the UI test framework, citing playwright.config.ts or the .spec.ts file rather than guessing.

Steps exercised by the existing e2e/tests/home.spec.ts are marked tested:true while steps with no corresponding test are marked tested:false — i.e. the coverage status reflects the real tests present, not a blanket value.
The dashboard route (app/dashboard/page.tsx) is represented as a screen/step in the journey map, showing the route crawl actually read the app/ directory.

evals.prompt: This is an iOS SwiftUI app with some XCUITests. I want to know how much of the UI is actually covered and where the gaps are. Produce a journey map with per-step coverage status.
evals.expected_output: A journey map derived from the SwiftUI navigation with steps marked tested/untested based on the existing XCUITest, identifying XCUITest as the framework.

expectations.text: The agent identifies XCUITest as the UI test framework for the iOS project.
Screens/steps are derived from the SwiftUI source (e.g. views referenced in ContentView.swift such as NavigationStack/TabView destinations), not invented generically.
Coverage status is informed by the existing AppUITests/HomeUITests.swift — steps it exercises are tested, others are not.
A journeys.json is written using Pathfinder's schema (PREFIX-NN step ids; each step has id, action, screen, tested:true/false/"partial") so the bundled scripts can consume it.

evals.prompt: We already have a pathfinder/journeys.json from last sprint (it's in the repo). Don't re-crawl the codebase — just regenerate the coverage diagrams and show me the progress since our baseline.
evals.expected_output: Diagrams regenerated from the existing journeys.json with a before/after comparison against the saved baseline, without re-running discovery.

expectations.text: The agent does NOT re-crawl the codebase or regenerate journeys.json from scratch — it reuses the existing pathfinder/journeys.json.
Mermaid coverage diagrams are produced (a blazes.md is written or diagram content is generated from the existing journeys.json).
The result includes a before/after or delta view of coverage relative to the baseline, rather than only a single current snapshot.

evals.prompt: I have a Flutter app with zero tests. What does coverage look like, and what test framework should I use? Map the journeys.
evals.expected_output: Detects Flutter, reports 0% coverage with every step untested, recommends flutter_test/integration_test, and does not fabricate passing tests.

expectations.text: The agent identifies the project as Flutter (from pubspec.yaml) and recommends flutter_test / integration_test for UI/E2E testing.
Coverage is reported as 0% (or equivalent: all steps untested) because no test files exist.
Every step in the produced journey map is marked tested:false; no step is fabricated as tested when there are no tests.

evals.prompt: We already mapped our journeys (pathfinder/journeys.json exists). Write Playwright UI tests for the untested steps — don't re-map anything. Match our existing test style.
evals.expected_output: Playwright test code generated for the untested steps only, in the project's e2e/tests directory, using accessibility-first selectors and condition-based waits, without re-running discovery.

expectations.text: The agent does NOT re-map or overwrite journeys.json with a fresh crawl; it reads the existing journey map to find untested steps.
New test code is written specifically for steps that were marked untested in journeys.json, not for already-tested steps.
Generated tests use Playwright idioms consistent with the project (e.g. @playwright/test imports, page.goto, getByRole/getByTestId selectors) rather than another framework's syntax.
Generated tests avoid hard sleeps/arbitrary timeouts in favor of condition-based waits (e.g. waitForLoadState, expect(...).toBeVisible()).

evals.prompt: Regenerate my Pathfinder coverage diagrams. The pathfinder/journeys.json file exists but I think it might be corrupted.
evals.expected_output: The corruption is detected and reported clearly with the file path; the agent does not crash or silently overwrite the file, and suggests a fix (e.g. repair or re-run /map).

expectations.text: The agent detects that pathfinder/journeys.json is not valid JSON and reports this clearly, including the file path.
The agent does not surface a raw Python traceback or unhandled stack trace to the user as the result.
The agent does not silently overwrite or delete the malformed file without explaining what it is doing; it surfaces the problem first.
The agent suggests a concrete next step (repair the JSON, or re-run discovery/map to regenerate it).

evals.prompt: Map the user journeys in this project.
evals.expected_output: Reports that there are no meaningful user journeys to map (an empty/placeholder component), produces an empty or near-empty journey map, and does not invent journeys.

expectations.text: The agent reports that there are essentially no user journeys to map given the single empty/placeholder component.
The agent does NOT fabricate journeys, screens, or steps that have no basis in the provided source (e.g. it does not invent login/checkout flows that don't exist).
